section.title: Zone one
section.p: The first sample zone uses a blue square beside a neutral gray marker.
Observers record the position, spacing, orientation, and visible outline.
Every sentence is original fictional material written for parser checks.
The sequence stays deterministic so source-size comparisons remain useful.

section.title: Zone two
section.p: The second sample zone pairs an amber circle with a narrow green line.
Observers read the labels from left to right and note the simple pattern.
No sample names a real product, person, location, or organization.
The content deliberately exercises repeated editable block structures.

section.title: Zone three
section.p: The third sample zone places two triangles above a short baseline.
One triangle points upward while the other points toward the right edge.
Each observation remains short enough for a reviewer to understand quickly.
The repeated shape provides a bounded workload for render-state builders.

section.title: Zone four
section.p: The fourth sample zone contains a dashed path around three small circles.
Each circle uses a different neutral shade and a distinct fictional label.
The path begins at the upper corner and ends near the lower boundary.
This section adds predictable depth without creating an oversized fixture.

section.title: Zone five
section.p: The fifth sample zone arranges four rectangles in a compact two-by-two grid.
Alternating outlines make adjacent markers easy to distinguish in the sample.
A short caption describes the grid without relying on private terminology.
The source stays stable and reviewable across repeated performance profiles.

section.title: Zone six
section.p: The sixth sample zone shows a curved path between a square and a triangle.
Small tick marks divide the path into five evenly described segments.
Nothing in the scenario represents an operational or commercial process.
The prose exists only to create a realistic but neutral editing workload.

section.title: Zone seven
section.p: The seventh sample zone repeats a circle, line, circle pattern three times.
Consistent spacing makes the repeated pattern suitable for visual comparison.
Simple vocabulary keeps the fixture understandable to any contributor.
The section count is fixed so element bounds catch accidental weakening.

section.title: Zone eight
section.p: The eighth sample zone closes the guide with one outlined hexagon.
A centered dot and two crossing lines complete the final fictional marker.
Reviewers can inspect the entire fixture without generated or copied content.
The final block keeps parse, render, and state payload measurements bounded.
